title: Route module CLI operations through the workspace front door

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: User feedback: host-facing agentic-planning and agentic-memory references should route through agentic-workspace; work grew beyond direct fix
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not expose direct module CLIs in ordinary host-facing guidance; keep package-local CLI identity and operation contracts only where they describe internal/package surfaces.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Add root workspace wrapper commands, update shipped and installed guidance, add guard tests, and leave deeper generated/package CLI migration to tracked follow-up issues.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A fix requires replacing package CLIs entirely, changing generated command package architecture, or rewriting historical archived planning evidence.
canonical_core.next_action: Commit and push the validated root front-door module CLI routing work to master.
completion_criteria: Route module CLI operations through the workspace front door is implemented, validated, and closed out honestly.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: User feedback: host-facing agentic-planning and agentic-memory references should route through agentic-workspace; work grew beyond direct fix

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Host-facing planning and memory operations can be invoked through agentic-workspace front-door subcommands while package-local CLIs remain internal/debug surfaces.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Removing package-local CLI entry points entirely; that is a deeper package architecture decision tracked by filed follow-up issues.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Current host-facing docs also contained direct module CLI references and needed cleanup, not only skills and startup recommendations.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: workspace, planning, and memory test/lint suites passed; contract and inventory guardrails passed; targeted current-doc scan found no direct module CLI guidance.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: None for this slice. The strict source-payload operational install checker still reports pre-existing planning bootstrap payload drift unrelated to this routing change.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Follow issues #926 and #927 if the package architecture should remove or further constrain module CLI entry points.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Root workspace CLI/contracts/reporting, planning and memory shipped skills, installed skill mirrors, workflow guidance, tests, and this execplan/state record.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 45 files; stop before expanding into generated command packages or historical archives.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; make test-planning; make lint-planning; make test-memory; make lint-memory
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing package CLI existence, generated CLI package semantics, or historical archive data.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated Python/TypeScript command packages, package-local parser names, lockfiles, or unrelated docs not needed for host-facing guidance.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.

stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

execution_run.what happened: Work started as a direct fix, then was promoted after scope expanded; issues #926 and #927 were filed, root planning and memory front-door wrappers were added, host-facing guidance was rewritten, and guard tests/contracts were updated.
execution_run.scope touched: root CLI/contracts/reporting, shipped planning/memory skills, installed skill mirrors, workflow and current docs, package memory bootstrap README, payload sync checker guidance, tests, and planning state.
execution_run.changed surfaces: agentic-workspace now exposes planning and memory front-door subcommands; host-facing current guidance points to agentic-workspace; compact routing output preserves required decomposition and maintainer-mode fields without breaking tiny payload budgets.
execution_run.validations run: make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; make test-planning; make lint-planning; make test-memory; make lint-memory; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py packages/planning/tests/test_install.py; targeted rg current host-facing docs for direct agentic-planning/agentic-memory invocations returned no matches.
execution_run.result for continuation: No continuation required for this slice. Follow issues #926 and #927 for deeper package CLI architecture if desired.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. closure_check.why this decision is honest: The requested host-facing routing fix is implemented and validated; broader removal of package-local CLIs is intentionally tracked separately.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Passed workspace/planning/memory test and lint suites, contract tooling, structured inventory, and no-match scan for current host-facing direct module CLI guidance.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A current host-facing workflow again recommends agentic-planning or agentic-memory directly for ordinary repo work.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Direct module CLI references existed beyond startup/skill surfaces, including current docs and payload-sync operator guidance.
signals fixed: Current host-facing docs and the payload sync checker now point to agentic-workspace front-door commands.
signals dismissed: Historical archives, package identity metadata, package-local tests, and source labels intentionally retain module package names.

discard: Historical archived references and package identity strings were left unchanged.
continuation: Use issues #926 and #927 for deeper package CLI architecture decisions.
